names.thesis: Alamos cut Q2 2026 guidance after seismic events and a storm-related power outage cut Young-Davidson output, pushing management to guide full-year 2026 production below the low end of prior guidance and costs above plan, and RBC responded by cutting its price target to $42 from $52 (still Outperform); layered on top of a short-medium bearish gold backdrop (spot down 14% over 3 months on a hawkish Fed), the operational miss makes AGI unattractive for new long exposure right now despite a Street-average buy rating and $59 mean target.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results and revised full-year guidance due with report after close July 29, 2026
Potential stabilization/recovery of Young-Davidson mining rates in H2 2026 as management reviews the mine sequence
Island Gold and La Yaqui Grande production tracking in line with plan
risks: Consolidated 2026 production guided below the low end with costs above guidance
Further seismic or infrastructure disruption at Young-Davidson
Broader gold price downside given hawkish Fed backdrop

evidence.note: Alamos guides full-year 2026 consolidated production below low end of guidance and costs above guidance after Young-Davidson shortfall
Alamos down 2.9% after-hours after lowering Q2 guidance on Young-Davidson seismic events and storm-related power outage
RBC cuts Alamos price target to $42 from $52 while maintaining Outperform; Street average rating buy, mean target $59.22

names.thesis: Algoma's own Q2 2026 guidance shows adjusted EBITDA of only CA$5-15 million, and even that figure is propped up by a CA$45 million one-off insurance settlement and a CA$50-55 million capacity utilization adjustment, while management explicitly flags tariffs as a 'structural headwind' still weighing on total shipment volumes even as record plate sales and EAF ramp-up continue; this matches the backdrop view that ASTL is a steel-long-sector outlier hurt by 50% US tariffs, so despite a constructive commodity backdrop and encouraging recent steel price gains, underlying core profitability looks weak and tariff-exposed.
catalysts: Second EAF unit coming online in H2 2026, completing the transformation plan
Pivot to a more Canada-centric strategy to reduce US tariff exposure
Recent rise in steel prices cited by management as encouraging
risks: US tariffs remain a structural headwind to shipments and margins
Adjusted EBITDA guidance is largely non-operational (insurance settlement + utilization adjustment)
Total shipment volumes still weighed down by broader market conditions

evidence.note: CEO Marwah says tariffs remain a structural headwind even as record plate sales and EAF ramp continue
Q2 2026 guidance: 175,000-180,000 tons shipped, adjusted EBITDA CA$5-15 million including CA$45M insurance settlement benefit and CA$50-55M capacity utilization adjustment
Confirms Q2 EBITDA guidance CA$5-15 million and shipment range, driven largely by one-time items

names.thesis: B2Gold reiterated 2026 consolidated guidance of 820,000-970,000 oz after producing 979,604 oz in 2025 and generating $3 billion in revenue, and cash grew to $479.4 million from $380.4 million at year-end, but the stock is down 7.5% since its last earnings report and the Goose Mine crushing-circuit fire (~$7 million repair, completion targeted Q3 2026) is an operational overhang; combined with the short-medium bearish gold backdrop, this nets out to a neutral stance — solid balance sheet and guidance reiteration offset by cost/repair friction and macro headwinds.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results due after close August 6, 2026, with call August 7
Goose Mine crusher repairs and phase-one upgrade completion targeted Q3 2026
Fekola Regional mining start pending exploitation permit
risks: Goose Mine fire repair and upgrade costs (~$18 million combined) and ramp delay risk
Gold price downside under a hawkish Fed backdrop
Operations concentrated in Mali, Philippines, Namibia carrying jurisdictional risk

evidence.note: B2Gold reiterates 2026 production guidance 820,000-970,000 oz; cash rose to $479.4M from $380.4M; Goose Mine fire repair ~$7M targeted for Q3 2026 completion
2025 consolidated gold production 979,604 oz including Goose pre-commercial ounces; $3B revenue, $1B paid to governments
B2Gold to release Q2 2026 results August 6, 2026 with conference call August 7

names.thesis: BofA cut its Buenaventura price target to $12 from $14 (Neutral) after Q1 2026 revenue missed estimates ($624.6M vs $651.5M expected), and flagged the stock as likely to stay range-bound near-term pending resolution of financing concerns ahead of 2026 catalysts; despite improved silver (+6% YoY) and gold (+8% YoY) production and a large Q1 net income jump to $335.4M, copper output fell 11% YoY, and the silver price backdrop is short-low (halved from January's $115 squeeze) — consistent with the existing short thesis on BVN.
catalysts: San Gabriel ramp-up continuing to lift gold output
Resolution of financing concerns cited by BofA as a prerequisite for re-rating
2026 catalysts referenced by BofA (unspecified timing)
risks: Silver price backdrop short-low after halving from January highs
Revenue miss vs consensus in Q1 2026
Financing overhang keeping the stock range-bound per BofA
Copper production declined 11% YoY on ore-processing mix shift

evidence.note: BofA cuts BVN price target to $12 from $14, Neutral; Q1 revenue $624.6M missed $651.5M estimate; silver production +6% YoY, copper -11% YoY
Q1 2026 net income surged to $335.39M from $140.09M a year prior; dividend yield 3.23% on a 29.2% payout ratio
Buenaventura described as Peru-based diversified precious/base metals miner with major revenue anchors at Uchucchacua and Cerro Verde equity stake

names.thesis: RBC raised its long-term uranium price forecast to $110/lb from $100/lb and lifted its Cameco price target to C$175 from C$160 (Outperform) citing sovereign and utility demand and contract settlement prices above term prices, while Q1 2026 uranium segment adjusted EBITDA rose to $423M from $286M YoY and 2026 attributable production guidance of 19.5-21.5M lbs was maintained even through the Cigar Lake suspension and earlier McArthur River/Key Lake flooding disruptions; the uranium demand backdrop (nuclear/AI power) is constructive, but CCJ already trades at 93.5x trailing earnings, so valuation caps the attractiveness despite strong fundamentals.
catalysts: RBC raised long-term uranium price forecast to $110/lb and price target to C$175
Sustained sovereign/utility uranium demand tied to new reactor projects
Westinghouse stake providing broader nuclear value-chain exposure beyond mining
risks: Stock trades at 93.5x trailing earnings, pricing in significant optimism
Second operational disruption this year (Cigar Lake suspension, following McArthur River/Key Lake flooding)
Uranium sector still 10-30% below moving averages despite the demand narrative

evidence.note: RBC raises long-term uranium price forecast to $110/lb from $100/lb, raises CCJ target to C$175 from C$160, Outperform
Q1 2026 net earnings $131M, adjusted EBITDA $509M, uranium segment EBITDA $423M vs $286M YoY; valuation at 93.5x trailing earnings flagged as main risk
Cigar Lake suspension noted as second 2026 operational disruption after McArthur River/Key Lake flooding; 2026 guidance of 19.5-21.5M lbs attributable maintained

names.thesis: Critical Metals' Tanbreez rare-earth project remains pre-revenue with first ore not expected until Q4 2028/Q1 2029 and concentrate export in Q3 2029, and the company burned $14.5M in cash in FY2025 ending with just $7.3M on hand, but a large capital raise (AUD293.5M from share disposals) cut FY25 net loss 56% YoY and boosted equity 20x to AUD174.26M, and management just launched a strategic process to monetize non-core assets (the Molulu Copper/Cobalt project in the DRC) to sharpen the balance sheet; this fits the long rare-earths backdrop (NdPr +74% YoY, supply gap) but the multi-year runway to first production means this is a financing/execution story, not a near-term earnings one.
catalysts: Strategic process to monetize non-core Molulu Copper/Cobalt DRC asset, announced July 8, 2026
Continued fundraising after AUD293.5M raised via share disposals in FY2025
Rare earth (NdPr) price strength supporting long-term Tanbreez economics
risks: Pre-revenue until at least Q4 2028/Q1 2029; concentrate export not until Q3 2029
Continued cash burn ($14.5M in FY2025) requiring further dilutive financing
Small-cap, foreign private issuer with limited quarterly disclosure

evidence.note: Tanbreez first ore not targeted until Q4 2028/Q1 2029, concentrate export Q3 2029; FY2025 cash burn $14.5M, ended with $7.3M cash
FY25 net loss AUD96.79M, down 56% YoY; equity up 20x to AUD174.26M on AUD293.5M raised from share disposals
Critical Metals announces strategic process to unlock value of non-core assets including Molulu Copper/Cobalt project in DRC

names.thesis: Denison remains an early-stage uranium developer generating only ~CA$4.6M in mining segment revenue against a CA$4.1B market cap, with Q1 2026 losses narrowing to C$0.02/share from C$0.05 a year earlier; its flagship Wheeler River project (95% interest) and toll-milling arrangement at McClean Lake give it real optionality into the constructive uranium/nuclear demand backdrop, but with minimal current cash flow this is a growth-and-financing story rather than a near-term earnings one, keeping the name roughly balanced between the sector tailwind and its pre-production stage.
catalysts: Wheeler River project advancement toward development decision
McClean Lake toll milling of Cigar Lake ore providing incremental revenue
Sector-wide uranium demand tailwind from nuclear/AI power buildout
risks: Minimal current revenue (~CA$4.6M) relative to CA$4.1B market cap reflects early-stage status
Uranium equities broadly trading 10-30% below moving averages despite bullish narrative
Continued net losses, though narrowing YoY

evidence.note: Q1 2026 adjusted loss narrowed to C$0.02/share from C$0.05 YoY; revenue $1.1M, down from $1.4M YoY
Denison described as early-stage Athabasca Basin developer with ~CA$4.6M mining segment revenue and CA$4.1B market cap
Denison holds 95% interest in Wheeler River plus interests in McClean Lake and Midwest joint ventures across ~457,000 hectares in the Athabasca Basin

names.thesis: Eldorado achieved first copper concentrate at its McIlvenna Bay project on June 7, 2026, with commercial production targeted for Q3 2026, a genuine diversification catalyst into copper (a long commodity per the current backdrop) alongside the Skouries project in Greece, but BofA maintains an Underperform rating (price target raised only modestly to $33 from $31, versus a Street mean of $44.62) and the broader gold price backdrop remains short-medium bearish, leaving the name balanced between a real project milestone and weak sell-side sentiment.
catalysts: McIlvenna Bay commercial production targeted Q3 2026, diversifying revenue into copper and zinc
Skouries project in Greece progressing toward high-margin free cash flow generation
Q2 2026 results due July 30, 2026 with call July 31
risks: BofA maintains Underperform rating despite a modest price target increase
Gold price backdrop short-medium bearish on hawkish Fed
Ramp-up execution risk at McIlvenna Bay toward nameplate 4,900 tonnes/day

evidence.note: First copper concentrate produced at McIlvenna Bay June 7, 2026; ramp toward 4,900 tpd nameplate, commercial production targeted Q3 2026
BofA raises Eldorado price target to $33 from $31 but maintains Underperform; Street average rating hold, mean target $44.62
Eldorado to report Q2 2026 results July 30, 2026 with conference call July 31

names.thesis: Endeavour Silver posted strong Q2 2026 volume growth — silver-equivalent production up 36% YoY to 3.4M oz, silver ounces up 31% and gold up 35% — driven by the Kolpa (Peru) and Terronera (Mexico) ramp-ups, with Terronera recoveries and grades in line with plan; this real production growth partially offsets the short-low silver price backdrop (down roughly half from January's squeeze highs), leaving the name roughly neutral as volume gains fight price headwinds.
catalysts: Terronera mine shifting into higher-grade areas in Q3 2026
Continued ramp-up of Kolpa operation acquired May 2025
Q2 2026 results due after close July 29, 2026 with call July 30
risks: Silver price backdrop short-low after halving from January highs
Guanaceví still relying on third-party mill feed for 30% of throughput
Currency exposure across Mexican peso, Peruvian sol and other operating currencies

evidence.note: Q2 2026 silver-equivalent production 3.4M oz, +36% YoY, driven by Kolpa and Terronera ramp-ups
Terronera Q2 production in line with plan, recoveries 85.4% silver/72.6% gold; Guanaceví throughput +4% YoY with 30% third-party mill feed

names.thesis: Fortuna's Q2 2026 gold-equivalent production of 72,217 oz was roughly flat with Q1's 72,872 oz, management expects the Lindero mine to strengthen in H2 2026 to hit midpoint guidance, and the company returned $80.2M to shareholders via buybacks in Q2 alongside a strong Diamba Sud feasibility study ($1.0B NPV, 60% IRR at $3,500/oz gold assumption); however, Zacks currently rates the stock a Strong Sell (Rank #5) even after 30.9% one-year gains, and the silver/gold price backdrop is bearish, so fundamentals and sentiment are pulling in different directions.
catalysts: Séguéla process plant expansion construction decision expected in coming weeks
Diamba Sud advancing toward final investment decision after positive feasibility study
Lindero mine expected to strengthen in H2 2026 to reach guidance midpoint
risks: Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell) despite recent share price strength
Gold/silver price backdrop bearish on hawkish Fed
Séguéla and Diamba Sud expansion decisions still pending, adding execution/capital risk

evidence.note: Q2 2026 gold-equivalent production 72,217 oz; $80.2M returned to shareholders via buybacks; Diamba Sud FS shows $1.0B NPV, 60% IRR at $3,500/oz gold
FSM shares up 30.9% over past year but carries Zacks Rank #5 Strong Sell
Lindero Mine positioned for stronger H2 2026 to reach midpoint of 2026 production guidance

names.thesis: Ivanhoe Electric's preliminary feasibility study shows meaningful leverage to copper prices — $1.4B after-tax NPV8% and 20% IRR at $4.25/lb copper, with NPV rising $240M per $0.25/lb increase in copper price — directly aligned with the long-copper backdrop (tariff catalyst), and the company just amended its Saudi Arabian JV with Maaden to extend the operational timeline through July 2033 and grant more operating flexibility; this remains a pre-production developer, so the copper price leverage is a multi-year call rather than a near-term earnings driver, and financing/execution risk should be flagged.
catalysts: Amended Maaden JV extending exploration/operational timeline through July 2033 with greater technical committee authority
Significant NPV leverage to copper price ($240M per $0.25/lb) underpinning long-copper thesis
Tunnel Boring Machine incorporation updating the 2025 feasibility study
risks: Pre-production developer with $1.24B initial capex requirement still to be financed
JV amendment triggered a 1.6% premarket share price decline
Copper price assumption ($4.25/lb) below the current spot backdrop level cited, but downside sensitivity still material

evidence.note: Ivanhoe Electric amends Saudi Arabian Maaden JV, extends timeline through July 2033, grants technical committees more fund-reallocation authority; shares down 1.6% premarket
PFS shows $1.24B initial capex, $1.32/lb cash costs, 20% IRR, $1.4B after-tax NPV8% at $4.25/lb copper, with $240M NPV sensitivity per $0.25/lb copper price change

names.thesis: Centrus signed a new $900M firm fixed-price DOE contract on June 30, 2026 to build out commercial HALEU enrichment capacity (plus options worth up to $170M more, $1.07B total contract value), completed all HALEU production under its prior demonstration contract two weeks ahead of schedule, and holds an existing LEU backlog of $2.4B; FY2026 earnings estimates have moved higher (three upward revisions, none lower, in the past two months) and the shares are up 6.41% over four weeks, all consistent with the constructive uranium/nuclear-AI-power demand backdrop, making this one of the stronger names in the group despite new capacity not coming online until 2029.
catalysts: New $900M DOE HALEU enrichment contract signed June 30, 2026, with up to $170M in additional options
Existing $2.4B LEU backlog providing revenue visibility
FY2026 consensus estimates trending higher with no downward revisions in two months
risks: First new HALEU capacity not expected online until 2029, a multi-year execution runway
Dependence on DOE contracting and government policy continuity
Piketon and Oak Ridge build-out requires successful large-scale capital execution

evidence.note: Centrus signs $900M firm fixed-price DOE HALEU contract (total value $1.07B with options), first new capacity expected online by 2029; existing LEU backlog $2.4B
LEU shares up 6.41% over four weeks; FY2026 consensus estimates revised up three times, zero times lower, over past two months
